headline: 1 Profitable Stock Worth Investigating and 2 We Brush Off

headline: Tesla is making just 250 final Model S cars at $159,420 each — and then it's gone forever

headline: Elon Musk’s X Launches Smart Cashtags for BTC, ETH, XRP, DOGE, MSTR, COIN, Others
summary: Elon Musk’s X has officially debuted Smart Cashtags for stocks and crypto. This new feature adds interactive, real-time financial data to users’ timelines. It supports major crypto and stocks cashtags such as BTC, ETH, XRP, SOL, DOGE, TSLA, MSTR, and COIN. The Smart Cashtags feature is currently available on iPhone in the US and Canada.

content.title: Elon Musk’s X Launches Smart Cashtags for BTC, ETH, XRP, DOGE, MSTR, COIN, Others

headline: Intel’s (INTC) Chip Business Comes Alive as Terafab Revives the Bull Case
summary: Intel (INTC) is seeing renewed momentum in its foundry business, with the Terafab partnership marking a potential inflection point and supporting a bullish view. The stock has surged nearly 50% in April, driven in part by its involvement alongside Elon Musk’s Tesla (TSLA), SpaceX, and xAI in the large-scale chip manufacturing initiative. headline: Tesla stock: 3 key things to watch into Q1 earnings
summary: Investing.com -- Barclays says investor focus heading into Tesla’s first-quarter earnings on April 22 will center on capital spending needs, the pace of the company’s transition toward physical AI, and near-term margin pressure, even as excitement builds around long-term projects.

headline: Tesla’s Robot Pivot And FSD Progress Test A High-Valuation Story
summary: Tesla (NasdaqGS:TSLA) is halting production of its Model S and Model X to convert factory capacity to build the Optimus humanoid robot. The company is planning mass production of Optimus at its Shanghai Gigafactory, targeting output of one million robots per year. Tesla’s Full Self-Driving (FSD) has received regulatory approval in the Netherlands and is under review for broader European clearance. Tesla has long been known for electric vehicles and energy products, but this pivot places...

headline: Tesla Has Some Robot Competition. The Stock Is Up.
summary: Tesla’s  humanoids aren’t for sale yet, but China’s Unitree Robotics has some options.  Unitree is now selling its cheapest humanoid robot on  Alibaba’s  AliExpress, according to Bloomberg.  The R1 Humanoid Robot lists for $6,820.40 and is available for preorder now.

headline: Alibaba Readies Robots for Foray Into Crowded Chinese Arena
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. plans to soon unveil its first robot, a four-legged device that will mark its entry into an increasingly crowded but popular arena.The Chinese company’s Amap unit is preparing to introduce a quadruped robot to the market that will compete with the likes of Unitree’s Go series, a company spokesperson said. Amap’s embodied intelligence research division is also exploring the feasibility of humanoid robots, a segment that’s captured Chinese consumer imagina

headline: Tesla’s Chinese Robot Rival Ramps Pre-IPO Global Push
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Unitree Robotics began selling its cheapest humanoid robot on Alibaba Group Holding Ltd.’s AliExpress on Wednesday, expanding its international presence in a white-hot race featuring heavyweight players like Elon Musk’s Tesla Inc.Hangzhou-based Unitree’s R1 model will cost roughly $8,150 with import fees factored in, and shipping to the US will be free of charge, the company said on Wednesday. It’ll also offer a scaled-down version priced at about $6,800. The R1 robots will be sol

headline: Elon Musk Hits Out At 'Tesla Killers' As EV Giant Outsells Rivals GM, Ford, Rivian In US
summary: Tesla Inc. CEO Elon Musk hit back at critics on Monday as the EV giant outsold all of its rivals in the U.S. domestic market during the first quarter of 2026. Tesla’s Q1 U.S. Sales In a post on the social media platform X on Monday, influencer Sawyer Merritt shared sales figures for almost all EVs available in the U.S. market, citing Cox Automotive data. The post shared how Tesla’s Model Y and Model 3 remained at the top of the sales charts with over 101,000 sales combined in the first quarter.

content.title: Elon Musk Hits Out At 'Tesla Killers' As EV Giant Outsells Rivals GM, Ford, Rivian In US
content.summary: Tesla Inc. CEO Elon Musk hit back at critics on Monday as the EV giant outsold all of its rivals in the U.S. domestic market during the first quarter of 2026. Tesla’s Q1 U.S. Sales In a post on the social media platform X on Monday, influencer Sawyer Merritt shared sales figures for almost all EVs available in the U.S. market, citing Cox Automotive data. The post shared how Tesla’s Model Y and Model 3 remained at the top of the sales charts with over 101,000 sales combined in the first quarter.
